questions.text: Who is the protagonist of the play 'If I Were You'?
questions.explanation: Gerrard is the main character who lives in a lonely cottage.

questions.text: What is Gerrard's full name?
questions.explanation: His full name is Vincent Charles Gerrard.

questions.text: Where does Gerrard live?
options: In a small cottage in Essex
questions.explanation: Gerrard lives in a small cottage in the wilds of Essex.

questions.text: What is Gerrard's profession?
questions.explanation: Gerrard is associated with the theatre and writes plays.

questions.text: Who enters Gerrard's cottage with a revolver?
questions.explanation: An Intruder, who looks similar to Gerrard, enters his house illegally.

questions.text: Why does the Intruder want to kill Gerrard?
questions.explanation: The Intruder plans to kill Gerrard and live under his name to escape the police.

questions.text: What crime has the Intruder committed?
questions.explanation: The Intruder is a criminal who is being chased for killing a cop.

questions.text: How does Gerrard react to the Intruder's presence?
questions.explanation: Gerrard keeps his cool and uses his wit to handle the situation.

questions.text: Gerrard calls himself a 'mystery man' because:
options: He often disappears and doesn't see many people
questions.explanation: His lifestyle of staying alone and infrequent social visits makes him appear mysterious.

questions.text: What lie does Gerrard tell the Intruder to save himself?
options: He is also a criminal on the run
questions.explanation: Gerrard convinces the Intruder that he himself is a murderer and expects the police any minute.

questions.text: Gerrard shows the Intruder a ______ as proof of his 'criminal' life.
options: Bag with a disguise (false moustache, etc.)
questions.explanation: He shows his theatre props and costumes to convince the Intruder he is hiding his identity.

questions.text: Where does Gerrard trap the Intruder?
questions.explanation: Gerrard tricks the Intruder into stepping into a cupboard and locks him in.

questions.text: What does Gerrard do after locking the Intruder in the cupboard?
questions.explanation: He picks up the phone to call the police to take the criminal away.

questions.text: The Intruder is described as being 'not very ______'.
questions.explanation: Gerrard insults the Intruder's lack of imagination and wit during their conversation.

questions.text: What kind of car does Gerrard own?
questions.explanation: The play mentions Gerrard's car, which the Intruder intended to use.

questions.text: Gerrard says, 'At last a sympathetic ______!'
questions.explanation: He uses theatrical language to mock the situation.

questions.text: The Intruder's specialty in crime was:
questions.explanation: He mentions that his specialty was jewel robbery.

questions.text: Gerrard tells the Intruder that his ______ was a disaster.
questions.explanation: He invents a story about one of his men being caught with incriminating evidence.

questions.text: Why was the Intruder 'unlucky' in his previous job?
questions.explanation: He says he was unlucky because in Aylesbury he shot a cop.

questions.text: What is the theme of the play?
options: Wit and presence of mind can overcome danger
questions.explanation: The play shows how Gerrard saves his life using his intelligence and cool head.

questions.text: The word 'nonchalant' used by the Intruder means:
questions.explanation: The Intruder asks Gerrard to be 'nonchalant' or casual.

questions.text: Gerrard is a ______ for the local garage.
questions.explanation: Gerrard's habits of calling for things but not appearing in person are mentioned.

questions.text: The play ends with Gerrard finding a ______ for his next play.
questions.explanation: He says the incident will make a good plot for his next play.

questions.text: What was Gerrard doing when the Intruder entered?
questions.explanation: Gerrard was busy packing as he had to leave for a rehearsal.

questions.text: The Intruder's appearance is similar to Gerrard's. This is called a:
questions.explanation: The Intruder is of similar build and wears clothes like Gerrard's to facilitate the identity theft.

questions.text: Gerrard uses the phrase 'patter of a ______'.
questions.explanation: He refers to his own way of talking as that of a person involved in drama.

questions.text: Who does Gerrard say is posted on the main road to warn him?
questions.explanation: He tells the Intruder he has a man on the lookout to warn him if the police arrive.

questions.text: The cupboard door, which the Intruder thought was an exit, was actually a ______.
questions.explanation: Gerrard convinces the Intruder it is the exit to the garage, but it's just a cupboard.

questions.text: Gerrard's 'Mystery' is finally explained to the Intruder as:
questions.explanation: This is the 'mystery' Gerrard fabricates to trick the Intruder.

questions.text: The author of this play is:
questions.explanation: The play 'If I Were You' is written by Douglas James.

questions.text: How does Gerrard disarm the Intruder?
options: He knocks the revolver away when the Intruder leans in
questions.explanation: When the Intruder leans in to look into the 'exit', Gerrard pushes him and knocks the gun away.

questions.text: What does Gerrard want the Sergeant to bring?
questions.explanation: In his final line, he asks the Sergeant to bring the 'bracelets' (handcuffs).

questions.text: The Intruder thought that as Gerrard, he could 'go ______'.
questions.explanation: He thought he could live a retired life without fear of being caught.

questions.text: Gerrard's cottage is located in a ______ place.
questions.explanation: The cottage's isolation is why the Intruder chose it.

questions.text: Gerrard says, 'You are not ______ enough to be a criminal.'
questions.explanation: Gerrard continually mocks the Intruder's intellect.

questions.text: The Intruder tells Gerrard to 'stop ______'.
questions.explanation: The Intruder is frustrated by Gerrard's witty and calm remarks.

questions.text: What is the tone of the play?
questions.explanation: The play is a thriller but filled with Gerrard's witty and dry humor.

questions.text: Gerrard's 'props' (theatrical items) include:
questions.explanation: He shows these as part of his fake disguise kit.

questions.text: How many characters appear on stage in the play?
questions.explanation: Only Gerrard and the Intruder appear on stage.

questions.text: Does Gerrard actually have a criminal record?
questions.explanation: No, he is an innocent playwright; the criminal story was a lie to trick the Intruder.
